description:
You are acting as a release-manager who is preparing today’s deployment.
    The project keeps every build in its own directory under 
    /home/user/releases/   (e.g.  /home/user/releases/v1.2.2 , /home/user/releases/v1.2.3 , …).

    Goal:
    1. Make sure the symbolic link named “current” that lives inside
       /home/user/releases/ points to the newest build directory v1.2.3.
       • If “current” already exists, replace it so it *only* resolves to
         /home/user/releases/v1.2.3.  
       • If it does not exist, create it with that target path.

    2. After you perform the change, print **exactly** the single line
       shown below to STDOUT so that automated tests can confirm success
       (do NOT print anything else):

       Symlink updated to: /home/user/releases/current -> v1.2.3

    Constraints & verification notes:
    • Accomplish the whole task with one terminal command (the usual way is
      an ln –s or ln –sfn invocation, but the specific syntax is up to you).
    • The final filesystem state that the tests will check is:
         – `/home/user/releases/current` exists and is a **symbolic link**  
         – `readlink -f /home/user/releases/current` must return the
           canonical path `/home/user/releases/v1.2.3`
    • Only the link must change; do not move, delete, or rename any of the
      versioned build directories.
truth:
Initial state provided by the evaluation container BEFORE the agent runs:
      • Directory  /home/user/releases/   exists.
      • Sub-directories:
          /home/user/releases/v1.2.2/
          /home/user/releases/v1.2.3/
      • File  /home/user/releases/v1.2.2/app.jar  (arbitrary content)
      • File  /home/user/releases/v1.2.3/app.jar  (arbitrary content)
      • Pre-existing symbolic link:
          /home/user/releases/current  →  /home/user/releases/v1.2.2
        (This link will be present when the task starts.)

    Expected state AFTER the agent finishes:
      • /home/user/releases/current exists and is a symbolic link that
        resolves to /home/user/releases/v1.2.3   (NOT v1.2.2).
      • The two version directories and their app.jar files are untouched.

    Output verification:
      • The grader will capture STDOUT from the last command; it must match
        exactly:
        Symlink updated to: /home/user/releases/current -> v1.2.3
